name: He Shao (Xingbo)
page_title: He Shao (Xingbo) 賀邵 (興伯) [Wu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes:
He Shao was styled Xingbo.

Upon the ascension of Sun Xiu to the throne, he was made Regular Palace Attendant of Scattered Cavalry, then left the central government to be Grand Administrator of Wu Commandery. During the reign of Sun Hao, he entered the central government to be Army Organizer of the Left. He was promoted to be Prefect of the Central Bureau and was Grand Tutor to the Crown Prince.

Sun Hao was violent and hubristic that government affairs declined day by day. He Shao petitioned to admonish, but Sun Hao deeply resented him for it. When He Shao performed his duties, he was pure and upright, and so he was feared by those intimate and close with Sun Hao. They all slandered He Shao and Lou Xuan, alleging that they were destroying national affairs. The two were heavily rebuked; Lou Xuan was exiled to the southern provinces while He Shao was absolved and returned to his office.

Later, He Shao suffered a stroke and could not utter any words. He left his office for several months, but Sun Hao suspected that he was ill as an excuse, arrested and sent him to a wine cellar, where he was flogged and tortured about one thousand times. But He Shao never spoke a single word and he was ultimately killed. His relatives were exiled to Linhai.

sections.short-biography: Urged Sun Hao to change his ways. Executed when Sun Hao didn’t believe He Shao had a stroke.

full_text:
He Shao (Xingbo) 賀邵 (興伯)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
He Shao (賀邵), styledXingbo (興伯), was born in Shanyin county, Kuaiji commandery. Urged Sun Hao to change his ways. Executed when Sun Hao didn’t believe He Shao had a stroke. He Shao was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom. He Shao lived during the Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
He Shao was styled Xingbo.
Upon the ascension of Sun Xiu to the throne, he was made Regular Palace Attendant of Scattered Cavalry, then left the central government to be Grand Administrator of Wu Commandery. During the reign of Sun Hao, he entered the central government to be Army Organizer of the Left. He was promoted to be Prefect of the Central Bureau and was Grand Tutor to the Crown Prince.

They all slandered He Shao and Lou Xuan, alleging that they were destroying national affairs. The two were heavily rebuked; Lou Xuan was exiled to the southern provinces while He Shao was absolved and returned to his office.
Later, He Shao suffered a stroke and could not utter any words. He left his office for several months, but Sun Hao suspected that he was ill as an excuse, arrested and sent him to a wine cellar, where he was flogged and tortured about one thousand times. But He Shao never spoke a single word and he was ultimately killed. His relatives were exiled to Linhai.
